title: Deploying UAR Files on iSeries
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
text: Deploying UAR Files on iSeries On some platforms, Uniface may encounter performance problems with reading UAR files if they have been transferred using FTP. Symptom Warning: cannot optimize search in FileName ; scanning sequentially, see Uniface Documentation. Copy Cause Uniface UAR files are stored in standard ZIP format. To quickly locate and access an object in these files, Uniface searches the zip header, which is located in the last 22 bytes of the file (before the end-of-file marker). If the zip header cannot be found in the expected location, Uniface reports the warning and reverts to a global scan of the zip file, which can severely degrade performance if the file is large. Physical file members on iSeries have a fixed record length. It can be anything, but for binary files it is usually 92 bytes. When a binary file is transferred int a physical file member from a Windows or Unix environment, then the last record is filled out with zeros. The zip header is located at the end of the data stream that was transferred, but because the last record is filled out, it is no longer in the last 22 bytes of the member but further back. As a consequence, the start of the zip header is not found at the expected position. When transferring into the iSeries IFS, there is no such problem. Neither does this problem exist when Uniface itself writes an archive into a physical file member, because Uniface marks the end of the data in the last record before the trailing zeros. Resolution To prevent this problem from occurring, you can do one of the following: Configure Uniface to write the UAR file directly to the platform via a Uniface Server acting in the role of file server. FTP the UAR files into the IFS or instead of into a physical file on iSeries. Use the Uniface Resource Manager's file and directory operations to copy a UAR into a physical file member so that the end of the data will be marked: CALL URM PARM('copy' ' SourceFile :*' ' DestinationFile :') DestinationFile must not exist. The colons and asterisk ( :* and : ) are crucial, if you omit these, the file is copied without changing the last record. Related Links Uniface Server A Uniface Server is a server-based process that handles requests for remote data and component execution that it receives from client applications. urm Executable for the Uniface Resource Manager, a command line utility for creating and manipulating Uniface archive files and other resources, files and DBMS tables. File Access on the iSeries File access on the iSeries occurs via a Uniface Server (USERVER) of type ANY or FIL. UAR Files A Uniface Deployment Archive (UAR) file is a zip file with a .uar extension that contains the application's runtime objects in a standardized directory structure. Was this topic helpful?
